schema.howto_name: How to Spot the Most Faked Crystals

howto_steps.name: Learn the most-faked list
howto_steps.text: Amethyst, citrine, turquoise, lapis, moldavite, malachite, jadeite, obsidian, clear quartz, rose quartz lead the fake market.

howto_steps.name: Apply the species-specific test
howto_steps.text: Each stone has one headline signal (turquoise: matrix, lapis: pyrite flecks, jadeite: heft, moldavite: origin).

howto_steps.name: Cross-check price and naming
howto_steps.text: Bargain pricing on high-value species (jadeite, moldavite, large turquoise) is the strongest pre-purchase warning.

howto_steps.name: Use a 10x loupe for any transparent stone
howto_steps.text: Bubbles separate glass from natural quartz-family stones.

howto_steps.name: For valuable pieces, get a lab certificate
howto_steps.text: Jadeite, moldavite, and large turquoise warrant professional certification.

compliance.gentle_note: This guide covers the stones most often sold as imitations. Home tests help you spot common fakes, but for valuable specimens (jadeite, moldavite, untreated turquoise), a gemological lab report is the gold standard. Crystal meanings and energetic properties are separate from authenticity.
compliance.brand_tagline: We name our stones and sources — return to what's real.
